title: Telangana ramps up monsoon, water crisis preparedness - Magzter
article_text:
We use cookies to provide and improve our services. By using our site, you consent to cookies. Learn more about our privacy policy
Telangana Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy on Monday directed officials to coordinate with upstream states to secure Telangana’s rightful share of Krishna and Godavari river waters, warning that drought-like conditions forecast by the India Meteorological Department due to the El Niño phenomenon could pose serious challenges this year.
Reviewing the state's monsoon preparedness, the Chief Minister instructed irrigation officials to hold discussions with Maharashtra and Karnataka to ensure water releases are made on a pro-rata basis in accordance with allocations. He cautioned that Telangana could face difficulties if upstream states continue releasing water only after their reservoirs reach full capacity.
This story is from the June 02, 2026 edition of The Daily Guardian.
